nodes.text: Good evening. Take a moment to think about your day — not everything, just what stands out.
If you had to sum up today, how did it feel?
When something went well today, what do you think helped the most?
options: I planned ahead or thought things through
Others stepped in at the right time
It just worked out on its own

nodes.text: When things didn’t go as planned, what did you do first?
options: I looked for what I could still control
I waited to see if someone else would help
I kept going without changing much

nodes.text: Think of one challenge today. What did you do next?
options: Asked someone for help or input
Focused on why it was happening

nodes.text: There were moments where you took charge of how things turned out. Even small actions like adjusting your approach show you were shaping the situation, not just reacting to it.
A lot of today may have felt driven by circumstances. That’s real. But even then, there were small choices you made — noticing those can help you feel more in control over time.
Now let’s shift a bit — from how things happened to how you showed up for others.
Which of these feels closest to how you showed up today?
options: I helped someone even when I didn’t have to
I focused on getting my own work done
I felt I deserved more recognition
I noticed others weren’t doing enough

nodes.text: During your interactions, what were you mainly focused on?
options: Whether my work was being noticed
Whether others were doing their part

nodes.text: You leaned toward contributing — focusing on what you could give rather than what you might get. That often builds trust and influence over time.
Part of your attention was on what you expected in return. That’s natural. But even a small shift toward contribution can change how these situations feel and play out.
Let’s zoom out a little more — beyond your role to the bigger picture.
When you think about your day, who does it mostly revolve around?
What felt most meaningful by the end of the day?
options: Making progress on my own work

nodes.text: Your focus stayed mostly on your own experience. That’s completely valid, but sometimes looking beyond yourself can bring more clarity and reduce pressure.
You naturally included others in how you saw your day. That broader view often brings a stronger sense of purpose.

Looking back, you approached your day with a {axis1} sense of control, focused {axis2} in how you engaged, and had a {axis3} perspective on what mattered. Even small shifts in any of these can change how a day feels.
That’s it for today. Come back tomorrow and notice what changes.
